zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas Spencer of Defford於1332年出生在英國武斯特斯爾德福德時，他的父親Sir John De Spencer32歲，母親Lady Alice De Verell27歲。他和Joan Polard至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1398年2月5日在英國武斯特斯爾享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas Spencer of Defford于1332年出生在英国武斯特斯爾德福德时，他的父亲Sir John De Spencer32岁，母亲Lady Alice De Verell27岁。他和Joan Polard至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1398年2月5日在英国武斯特斯爾享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas Spencer of Defford was born in 1332, in Defford, Worcestershire, England, his father, Sir John De Spencer, was 32 and his mother, Lady Alice De Verell, was 27. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Joan Polard. He died on 5 February 1398, in Worcestershire, England, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas Spencer of Defford est né en 1332, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John De Spencer, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Lady Alice De Verell, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Joan Polard. Il est décédé le 5 février 1398, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas Spencer of Defford 1332 in Defford, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John De Spencer 32 und seine Mutter Lady Alice De Verell 27. Er hatte mit Joan Polard mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 5. Februar 1398 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Worcestershire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Spencer of Defford è nato il nel 1332 nel Defford, Worcestershire, England, il suo padre, Sir John De Spencer, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alice De Verell, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Joan Polard. Morì il 5 febbraio 1398, nel Worcestershire, England, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer of Deffordが1332年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードで生まれた時，父Sir John De Spencerは32歳，母Lady Alice De Verellは27歳であった。彼はJoan Polardとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1398年2月5日にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas Spencer of Defford(이)가 1332년 영국 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John De Spencer(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alice De Verell(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Joan Polard(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1398년 2월 5일 영국 워스스터실르에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas Spencer of Defford nasceu em 1332, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John De Spencer, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alice De Verell, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha com Joan Polard. Ele faleceu em 5 de fevereiro de 1398, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas Spencer of Defford родился в 1332 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John De Spencer было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Alice De Verell, 27. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Joan Polard. Он умер 5 февраля 1398 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas Spencer of Defford nació en 1332, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John De Spencer, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Lady Alice De Verell, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija con Joan Polard. Murió el 5 de febrero de 1398, en Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 66 años.
